power.name: Mana Leech
power.description: leeches the mana of your enemies.

power.name: Mind Thrust
power.description: pierces your enemies with the power of your mind.

description.text: It looks like a rice ball, smells like a rice ball, tastes like a rice ball and even feels like one, but the higher-ups say it's a jelly donut and that's what it'll be.
A bunch of table grapes. Why, you aren't going to gripe about groping grapes, are you?
The fruit of the wilds, or some would have you believe.
A slice of sweet pie filled with grape jam. Both children and adults enjoy eating it.
A sliver of concentrated mana with a chewy center encased by a delicious outer shell. Melts in your mouth, not in your hand.
It's boiled tofu in boiled greens, a vegan's paradise. Over or improper consumption may lead to nutritional deficiencies.
Even the Japanese can't save lutefisk, not with all the wasabi in the world.

Crunch-munch! Chew-chew! Snuffle-slurp! Crunch-munch! Chew-chew! Snuffle-slurp! Oh wait, there seems to be a note stuck in the bottom of the bowl; you'll have to eat your dinner to get to it. You've heard of messages written on the bottoms of glasses, but this is ridiculous.
Looking at this little piece of chewy candy, you can see how it might fill your belly, but the manufacturer's claims about it raising your IQ is another kettle of fish altogether.
Fills the Belly, but...
It's a slice of declicious cake, most definitely here and not a lie. Its conflicts with pie have been some of the bloodiest (and creamiest) throughout history.
A huge and delicious apple. You'd better not let the Guildmaster catch you eating this!
It's a dastardly combination of both cake AND pie, the eating of which will create a temporal paradox of rich deliciousness. You can still eat it if you're willing to brave the effects, though.
This round little loaf of bread was first invented in a quaint diner by the lakeside, and is brimming with inspration and knowledge. Eat it to learn one random recipe.

This dark little pill brims with the promise of cyanide and happiness. Feel this run is going nowhere? Know it's all going to end badly? Tired of this character and want to roll another one, yet can't bring yourself to play badly in order to die? Just swallow this pill and end it all. Alternatively, you could sell it to Brax and let some other chap do the whole dying thing.
Coloured a clear, light blue, this drink was used to keep up elven spirits during protracted skirmishes with the dwarves. Usually served in small thimbles, it promises refreshment one way or another.

A very sweet and thoroughly unhealthy drink, used by elven hippymancers to get around that pesky 'no alcohol outside of worship' restriction on their priestly class. Why are you even considering putting this anywhere close to your mouth?
It's a cup of Japanese rice wine. People can make alcohol out of pretty much anything, if they put their minds to it.
It's wine, only in a horribly expensive crystal wineglass. Or maybe it's just plastic - you can't tell.
Remember, kids. Eat your greens, drink your milk, brush your teeth and stay in school. Health-conscious wizards often spike this with rum, or even worse, sherry.

Tested, tried, and sworn for by a good portion of the Ankh-Morpork City Watch; this particular mug has 'YES I NEED THIS MUCH COFFEE' printed on the side. It's like eating an electrical storm and gives you the energy to do what has to be done (and save your brother if need be), only with the crash afterwards.
A marvellous blend of fruit flavours swirls in this dessert wine.
A tempest in a bottle.
Traditionally brewed by the rangers of Moonshade, ice wine is quite capable of literally chilling you to the bone.
A staple of the average muscle diggle's diet. Main ingredients include whey, meat solids, and gluten.
Usually made by boiling up an anchor and straining off the residue.
Often found in suspicious sports drinks; much like a sugar rush, only ten times worse.
Needed for strong bones and a strong brain. Not an acceptable substitute for booze, though.
Zinc is an important mineral that helps build one's immunity to all sorts of nasty effects.
A powerful herbal tonic, best taken when reduced to a syrup after boiling forty times in the traditional Britannian way. Tastes quite disgusting, though.
Swirling mildly in its stoppered vial, this potion promises refreshment without healing, much like a diet cola.
They're here. Does anything and everything.
A shot of dubious quality. The label on the side declares that this is an addictive substance and overdosing is not recommended.

Short for Rancid Orcish Imitation Dietary Supplements, these nasty and addictive pills are handed out to Dredmor's assembly-line workers to make them work harder, faster, and on less sleep. Overdosing will mess up your body, so watch it.
When you bought this game, you were promised the chance to stuff aluminum powder up your nostrils. Now you can do that - and more. This is an addictive substance - overdosing will NOT help you.
A study aid commonly employed by wizardly students, this elixir once helped magicians in the field aim their fireballs better. The practice of spiking it with rum, though, put a quick end to that.

This discoloured and murky potion bubbles ominously in its flask; drinking it will turn your blood to acid. The higher your maximum health, the more blood gets splashed around when you are struck in melee.
This bubbling red potion makes your feet burn! Drink it and let your pursuers feel the heat!
This potion vibrates madly in its thin glass vial.
This viscous potion looks adequately thick and heavy in its small glass vial.
Nether flames roil within this deep crimson flask. Why do you want to set yourself on fire, anyways? Worried ninjas will catch you?
The jelly-like substance in this thin glass bottle is viscous and rubbery.
This deep crimson potion roils with pure life-force. It restores you based on your maximum health and mana values.
'Help help I've fallen into the dinner machine help help I can't keep running forever someone get me ou-' the note ends there in a streak of discoloured fluids.
'Looking for a good time? Call this number to hire a social escort from Red Lichess Society! Usual rates apply.'
A message is written here in a shaky hand. 'I know where you live, Eyebrowed One. Watch your back.'
It's the nutritional information pertaining to the meal you just ate. DSHA complicance regulations are well and alive in the dungeon, it seems.
'Your fortune for today is indeterminate. Keep away from large bodies of water and undead sorcerers; they spell trouble. If you ever had the urge to go on an eating binge, today is the day.'
'Have you given thanks to Obsidia today for partaking of the land's bounty?'
Don't tell anyone else, but FAXroom is really hidden on level 8 behind a false wall. Look for a small Krong altar room and examine the north wall directly behind the anvil.
Have you found a hidden shrine to Selzauide yet?
This note is horribly mangled and looks as if it's been through an evisceration chute. Come to think of it, where DID this dinner come from?
'No diggles were severely harmed in the making of this meal.'
'Unfortunately, today is not your lucky day. Keep away from traps you aren't sure you can disarm, stay away from corrupting monsters, and try not to open any evil chests unprepared.'
It's an advertisment, complete with a clip-out coupon redeemable at any B-Mart outlet. 'Mordor Mango - now made with 100 percent elf!'
It's an advertisment for Bolt Council marketables. You'd imagine they wouldn't really need advertising anymore, what with the consortium and all, but here it is.

There's some writing here, scrawled in a shaky hand. 'Don't eat the food here. Dredmor puts stuff in it; withdrawal from food may very well kill you in the end. Also, people around you may be habitual food users. Just look at them stuff their faces, and tell me that's not addiction.'
'Your fortune for today will be generally good, although you'll want to hoard crafting materials this run. Quite possibly a recipe may come up that requires some out of the way ingredient.'
The Roguish Renovator would like to thank you for playing his mod. If you want to know what's on his mind, try and find his secret room!
I like dragons. But you should've already figured that out, right?
'Today is indeed your lucky day! It's a good day! To die, that is, but nevertheless a good day!'

'If you are suffering from a constant desire to eat, hallucinations telling you to 'eat food to regain health' or hoarding large amounts of food for later, you may be suffering from food addiction. Please stop by the food addiction counsellor's group on level fifteen to learn more.'
'Your fortune for today: bad. Don't even think about Kronging anything. Play conservatively and watch your step.'
Apparently, this dinner was made with a hundred percent natural flavourings. That would explain the taste, yes.

It's an old set of notes on something called 'Bessel Equations'. You're sure that it's just plain old ink on paper and not human blood or something equally eldritch and sinister, but pure evil still drops from the inscriptions.
'Advice for the day: let sleeping liches lie.'
It's a print-out coupon redeemable at McDiggles' for a Dredmor Burger, Footy Fries and a Sauron Shake, complete with a Miasma Mousse dessert.'
It's an ancient treasure map pointing out the location of a great booty where X marks the spot. But...there are no Xes in the dungeon...
It's a scrunched-up sheet of paper with the following scrawled on it in ink: 'we will all die someday.' Grim.
'Over 9000 diggles die each year from malnutrition. Donate to the Food for Diggles program and help young diggles get a balanced diet of adventurer bits and pieces today!'

It's part of an old travel brochure. 'Welcome to Elfland! Stay awhile and visit the great monument tofuhenge, birthplace of the sacred order of hippymancers! See the natural beauty of the soy fields in full bloom!'
'What goes in a hot dog? Maybe I shouldn't ask...there's something about a tube of meat that makes it a delicious treat...
A tightly-sealed capsule, used to secure loot. Will you open it up by using it on yourself, or sell it as it is?
This lovely take-away hamper is often given away at Bolt Council community-liasion fairs. Open it up to find what's inside!
An old dragon skull, perhaps part of a long-rotted trophy. Whatever will you make with this?
Transmuted with the power of alchemy, this metal is highly thaum-conductive and does not occur in nature.
A scented prayer candle, of the kind dragons use. There's a blank space for you to etch in your own prayers, if you so desire.
This enormous, flat obsidian crystal gleams with an inner fire. You can't be sure if it really is a fallen scale from the draconic mother goddess, but it's definitely special in some way.
A sword blank made from a strange substance. It feels cold to the touch.
Also servicable as a fire poker, although not very well.
Hits only one time, not five.
A stick of charcoal that serves well enough for clobbering monsters. Also handy for starting a fire. Do not ignite with gasoline.
You didn't know there were any good fishing spots in the dungeon, or you'd have brought your cooler and tackle box.
This spear was said to be made for penetrating the veil of the heavens and thrusting deep into the starry unknown that lies beyond.
A thick green slime covers the shaft of this spear, and an equally foul stench rises from the edge. Are you REALLY sure you want to be using this thing?

To get one of these, you need to sail across half the world, venture across glacier-bound continents and into ancient ruins, and finally get an old woman to forge it for you after convincing her you're not going to kidnap her grandson. Or you could just get one off the corner store.
First conceived of by a dragon's personal dentist (who subsequently became very rich), the point of this spear is shaped from a dragon's tooth.

The tip of this spear has been fashioned from the point of a dragon's horn. Strange tingles run down your fingers as you grip the shaft - as you do battle with enemies, it has a small chance of allowing you to fight on tirelessly.
Like the Sword of Necropoking, only less slashy and more stabby to create an approximately equal level of grimness.
That must've been one heck of an organ, and a wierd one too to require such a fork.
This mysterious pole was used by a stone monkey in aiding a monk on a pilgrimage to the west to retrieve the Great Plot Coupon. According to legend, it can also extend and retract at will.
A magical lance of the sort knights throw at ghouls and ghosts, only considerably souped up and far less aerodynamic.

Pure earth magics blaze within the tip of this spear, clouding the obsidian's otherwise glassy surface with life-force. This is the sort of artifact that ends up being discovered centuries later amidst pleasant hills and meadows, used by the local halflings to grow pumpkins.
Looks like someone left this one out in the open for too long.
Based on the hero of a popular children's play, this knife has caused countless injuries to children attempting to imitate the 'Steel Samurai Sushi Slice'.
Like a shiv, only made out of POISON.
Like a shiv, only...well, you get the idea.
A leftover from the Dwarven Army surplus store, this enormous potato peeler does extra damage to veggies.
Lightweight, durable and resistant to corrosion - a very popular cutting edge with a variety of age groups and lifestyles indeed.
More show than sharp, these daggers are often worn at the hips of young noblemen who fancy themselves as dangerous assassins.
Their blades dark and dull, these daggers are said to be alive, with the power to drink the souls of those they kill.

A claw from the legendary werezompire, a beast so rare and elusive that no one knows where it is or what it even looks like. The best reports have it looking like everything, that is, looking just like a werezompire.
The blade of this dagger has been fashioned from an ancient dragon's tooth and has retained a small part of that power.
This nasty little dirk is smeared with clear acid and geared towards severing your opponent's nerves.

What's better than a knife fashioned from the crystalline teeth of the spawn of The Grey Worm That Burrows In The Sky? Why, THREE of such knives stuck together, of course! Also handy for draining mana from your foes.
A masterpiece of the artisan metalworker Lady Svetlana, the strange substance of this dagger is said to be imbued with her own blood, sweat and tears.

You now 'prosess' Digula's fang - albeit after a few modifications to make it easier to grip. Find an altar-lookish thingummy to toss it into along with the rest of Digula's bodyparts. Also drains blood from an enemy on hit.
This honed shard of obsidian is sharper than a knife's edge, darker than ink, and feels strangely invigorating in your hand.
This dagger is little more than a needle of primal ice affixed to a handle, and yet merely looking at its edge chills you to the bone as a golden sheen crackles along its blade.
A single line of runes is etched around the dagger's handle: 'My name is Eclipse. When the world is at its darkest, I shall appear.'

One of the seventeen great weapons, this dagger was forged from a cursed dagger, a golden chain, and the soul of a crane. It was wielded by an assassin in his nearly-successful quest against the gods who'd ruined his life.

In ancient days of ultimate yore when the hero Ever-Tar walked the land, properly-crafted glass swords were most deadly and pussiant things, killing anything they struck with but one blow and shattering afterwards. It's up to you to decide if this imitation model is better or not.
Because no fantasy game's complete without a flaming sword.

The hilt of this weapon of crystallised frostfire is shaped like a crown. Warning: DSHA regulations clearly stipulate that the manufacturer will be in no way held responsible for any injury or loss that may arise from attempting to put this sword on your head.
Mages love azure, and this weapon is no different; the blade sings as it whistles through the air. In a lovely alto, I might add.
This slender and elegant bone rapier gives you a small chance turn your dodges into counterattacks.
The edge of this blade drips with lush, green acid, the origin of which supposedly stems from the bad breath of chain-smoking plant monsters.

'I never cared about justice, and I don't ever recall calling myself a hero...I have always only fought for the people that I believe in. I won't hesitate...if an enemy appears before me, I will destroy it!'
Each blade of this three-bladed sword has a different edge. The blades look like they're arguing.
This gleaming sword cuts a swathe through your foes and reduces their defenses.
Earth! Wind! Water! Fire! No, you can't join in - what kind of lame power is Heart, anyway?

'The name's Arcadion,' the demon trapped within the blade growls. 'NOT Alastor. Remember that, and don't you dare say 'round trip' in my presence. So, what'll you be having tonight, fire or magic? I don't do death any more.'
This burning blade is one of Lady Svetlana's greatest masterpieces, almost without peer. Attack! Attack! Attack! Who needs defense?
If you stab someone and there is no one around to see it, did it really happen?
Sliced with the wind.
This lithe silvered sword slips in and out smoothly and easily.
This small, flat sword helps you block incoming blows. You have a small chance to defend yourself better after a successful block.
This sword, while not terribly sharp, has the disturbing property of being able to leech knowledge and experience by by mere contact.
Just holding this staff, you feel empty inside. Void. Like something's missing, something's being sucked out of you, gone forever and never to return...or maybe you just skipped lunch.
Why's the knob on this one so huge? Is it secretly overcompensating for something?

With all the work in recent years, what with the elves and dwarves and so many young people taking degrees in dungeoneering, Death's had to step up his game to deal with the increased workload. He's still got enough pride in his work not to turn to outsourcing, but has come up with a number of work-saving innovations, and this obsidian-bladed scythe is based on one of those.
Oh, hey! There's my ball!
Bonk! It has a chance of knocking your opponents silly with each hit.
An old, floppy fish. Parts of it are starting to peel and come off when you hit enemies with it.
Cold and spiky, perfect for keeping annoying neighbours away.
This impressive and hefty piece of equipment comes with its own natural lubricant and slips in and out easily. Perfect for fixing your steam-powered engines.

A reasonably fresh fish, reputed for leaping out of the water and clamping down on unlucky anglers' mouths. After rings fell out of popularity following the Mount Damn Incident, this lovely pink fish was used in marriage proposals for a while.
'The queen needs a new botox injection! Go forth and beat the taxes out of the peasants!'
This species of fish has been blessed by the Lutefisk God, blinging it out immensely, and is revered by its worshippers as having some small part of the god's divinity.
Made with the dead to fight the dead, this bony mace deals additional damage to evil creatures.
'Now you vermin will feel the full force of my birthright - the fury of the earth itself!'
If you fix this axe up right, the blades can be used as serviceable hot plates in a pinch.
The edge of this axe drips with blood. It has the ability to heal you with an enemy's life-force when you land a critical hit.
For felling demon trees. Don't ask.
Forged with dragon bone, this heavy axe allows you to use your critical hits to identify and expose your enemies' weaknesses.
An ancient Kanadian relic from the times of the first lumberjacks, who used neither steel nor flint, but ice to edge their axes (because everything was frozen).
An axe with a dark, sharp obsidian blade that seems to both gleam and draw in all light at the same time, no matter how impossible that may sound.

Used by Elves against Dwarves to great effect in the war, this bow is skilfully crafted from dead wood. As opposed to living wood, which has a tendency to whimper and moan most unsettlingly when one draws the bow.
This crossbow adds a high-impact blasting charge to all your bolts to help shred youe enemies' armour, leaving them open to further damage.
The hushed whispers of the dead echo in the bowstring of this grim bone bow, hungry for the breath of others.
Invented in the faraway land of Shin, the cartridge of this crossbow allows for the firing of four mini-bolts to land about the main target.
One of the few weapons to be banned from public sale by the Bolt Council, the Big F-ing Crossbow is a notorious mainstay of green-clad demon hunters for its sheer stopping power.
Painstakingly handcrafted by a Kanadian hermit with which to hunt the god-beavers of the far north, this makeshift crossbow freezes its bolts to slivers of ice before loosing them.

This crossbow was devised by Integron the Surface-Engineer, who wanted to prove conclusively that knowledge was indeed power. The bolts it fires are charged by the sheer power of your sagacity to deal additional damage.
Finally, someone's come up with one of these that DOESN'T involve tattoos.
This clear, armoured shell is wrought from alchemical processing of fossilized dragon bone to draw out every last iota of latent power.

This light yet sturdy bone cuirass is a little grim-looking. Sometimes, you hear old, deep voices in your head urging you to do things like not eating your greens or drinking your milk! Oh, the horror!
Infused with powdered dragonbone, this suit of armour shields you from forces both phyiscal and magical.
An elegant red suit complete with a cravat, perfect for the edgy prosecutor who goes the extra mile.
Doesn't ANYONE know that precious metals are most impractical for making armour?
A breastplate of arcanite over a steel support. Tens or perhaps even hundreds of metal farmers sacrificed their lives just to make this.
This breastplate is no obsidian scale cloak, being heavy and hefty, but will very definitely keep you cool through the deep breathing with all your extremities intact.
The Green Ranger was known most famously as the friend of the mighty dragon Zord, whom to summoned to his aid with a magic flute.
This nifty little lens fits over your eye well enough and ups your critical-hit ratio.
Not only does this nifty little lens fit over your eye well enough, it also protects you from horrible lens flare and makes your strikes more accurate.
This nifty little lens allows you to sometimes strike more than once in a turn.
The dull, white gleam in the sockets of this skull-turned-helm makes your enemies pause. Speak the words of power and send them flying!
A must-have stylish hood for the hip and cool cultist of today, elder being-approved. Good for being all ominous.
Now who's looking all fine and dandy today?
This obsidian helm is light yet sturdy, gleaming in the dungeon's light. A little hard to see out of, though.
Does being cool really mean having to bump into every single thing in the dungeon? Who cares? You're cool. Cool people don't care about bruises. Or pain. Or bleeding. Or death.
This mask of obsidian and volcanic ash bears a startling likeness to popular representations of the draconic god of earth.
A cheap and rough silver pendant of the kind often found in goblin night markets.
Aren't there supposed to be two of these? What kind of doomed idiot would wear only one?
This lustrous silk scarf has been meticulously sewn together by young diglet flippers in Dredmor's sweatshops.
Makes you feel all warm and fuzzy inside.
This ancient silver pendant is decorated with draconic runes honouring a goddess of wind and ice.
No star can glow with a broken heart.
Magical power overwhelming! Victory neither certain nor instant, though.
Wisps of light play over the reflective surface of this amulet, the sort of which priestly types hold out against the forces of darkness during the climactic scene of a horror movie.
The sun's radiance burns all to ash!
A brilliant light pulses at the heart of this clear glass orb.
Not quite as well-known as a heart container, but still as effective.
Wild, distortive energies lurk in this angular and sinister orb.
Frost and wind alike swirl madly in this orb of clear crystal.
Phoenix fire swirls within this clear crystal, the wild, unstable energies within begging to be released.
This weighty tale chronicles the adventures of Bronan and his many fist-bumping exploits with his bro the Muscle Wizard.
At a first glance, you think it's a strategy guide to the newest board game from Inferno Diversions, but upon closer examination it's just a gem-studded tome on smithing. Boo.
Another book by the code-wizard Null, this book promises to make you the master of winds in a sealed-off dungeon with no access to the sky.
Authored by the great code-wizard Null, this popular book is a must-have for diggers, delvers, and all other spleunky-types.

item.name: Shocking Revelations of a Charged Mind

description.text: This biography of the wizard Tesla focuses on his little-known youth, the young wizard's exploits as an apprentice and the beginnings of his love with all things electric.

This series of thick, scholary tomes is covered in crabbed handwriting by the archivist J-Factor, and holds everything you'll ever want to know about Dredmor and his minions. Unfortunately, there's simply so much that it'd take you more than your lifetime to read it all; this thick volume you hold proclaims itself as 'The guide to dangerous mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians, fish, jellyfish, insects, spiders, crustaceans, grasses, trees, mosses and lichens of the Dungeons of Dredmor, volume 29C, part three'.

The pages of this tome are utterly blank, and yet you can't help but get the feeling that's not all to it. Maybe you might remember something if you held it and someone knocked you really hard on the head?
This mysterious self-help book contains all sorts of instructions for homebrewing your own mental powers. Magnetic bangles sold separately.
This little-known volume by the science-mage Nobbel Nobbs even has a fuse stuck into the spine. Monsters might even ignite it when they strike you in combat...better keep it out of their reach.

Draconic runes are etched onto the borders of the shield: 'Because the wise amongst us realise that all nations, all peoples, even us, are no more to Her than any other, for everything will be gone in the end and new, different creatures will take their place. But She and the land is, was, and ever will be, until her father awakens, until the end of time.'
This shield of magic film won't block a blow very well, but it'll keep otherworldly forces at bay.
These large, reflective shields were traditionally assigned to Heliot dwarves, who used them to signal across the battlefield.
This shield of pure magical energy protects you from all sorts of supernatural oddities. It's sort of like a portable mystery machine.
This belt keeps your wizardly pants up and comes with a loop for hooking a bottle of your favourite vintage onto.
Contrary to popular belief, this sash won't keep you from fainting. It'll still help, though.
This belt is the true mark of a martial artist.
Braided from a fine weave of vines, this belt is the height of Argylian handicrafts. Quite flammable, though.

This is Mister Hip Flask. Mister Hip Flask wants to be your friend. He will never argue or reproach you, he will just be there when you need him. Mister Hip Flask will listen to all your sorrows and ease them. Mister Hip Flask can also be gotten without the dungeon collapsing, for he is not a load-bearing flask.
This mysterious sash grants its wearer the ability to survive falling from any height, so long as they land in a haystack. No one has any idea how it works, save that pigeons are somehow involved.
Aah, no better material to guard your vulnerables than awfully sharp volcanic glass, is there? You must be a very strong, brave and daring person!
Considering that fingers are often the most prone to frostbite, is it really a good idea to put this on?
This small ring has cheap runes of protection etched into it.

This signet ring is the traditional mark of the Ulmish Alchemists' Society, which seeks to phase out magic and smiles with with power of alchemy. Current reagents under study by the Society include unbrushed panda teeth, the sound of a puppy being kicked at midnight, and the left hand of a gnome caught cheating at cards.

Operating instructions - each turn, put one charge on Blue Mana Battery. To use, add one blue mana to your mana pool and remove as many charges as you wish. For each charge removed from Blue Mana Battery, add one blue mana to your mana pool. Play this ability as an interrupt.
An avian ninja warrior, much coveted by the Evil Ninja Empire. Collect all five to gain ultimate ninja power today!

While the most famous purveyors of coffee, the Monks of the Dark Bean view mere imbibing as a flawed path through which one's soul can touch the brew, commonplace and earthly. To get around that problem, the highest of their order are supplied with these devices to directly experience the rawest essence of their dark, bitter god.
A small hoop of dragonbone, polished to a shine. Somehow, wearing this makes you feel immensely powerful.
In another game, this would have been called an energy tank or piece of heart. Too bad you're stuck with this.
Ninja ninja? Ninja ninja ninja ninja, ninja ninja. Ninja ninja ninja! Ninja ninja ninja - ninja - ninja ninja.

'The Verse flows throughout Aquaria. Through each ripple and wave. Through every living being. The Verse binds us all as one. The Verse binds us, narrator and explorer. My story will become your own, and yours will become mine.'
This little trinket of gold on obsidian is a fount of wisdom in the palm of your hand.
Worn mostly by elven hippymancers during their ceremonies at Tofuhenge, these light boots are dyed with all-natural herbal essence dyes.
Covered in steel plates and weighted with concrete, these boots will give your feet a solid kick.
Because the Japanese do everything better, even boots to the head.
Walk a mile in someone's shoes, so the saying goes - never said anything about walking a mile in someone's bones.
Seems like a shame to track these all over the dirty dungeon floor.
Imbued with the dessicated and ground-up bodies of 100 percent free-range and organically-grown fairies.
Deft, nimble and with a velverty Darrskin lining that keeps your feet dry.
Despite being plated in obsidian, these boots are surprisingly light on your feet. You could cross lava on these, if it were hard enough to walk on.
They're magical!
These gloves protect your hands while you embrace nature by hacking and digging at it.
A steel bracelet, for those who're into that sort of thing. Y'know. Steel. Bracelets.
These gloves are stained with the remnants of countless alchemical experiments. They help you salvage Alchemical Ooze from blobbies and other slime monsters, even if you're not a rogue scientist.
For when you need to get a good grip on your enemies.
A silvered bracelet. Not quite blingy, but flashy nevertheless - no one's going to be taking out your wrists while you're wearing these.

A pair of green-dyed gloves, designed by the hero Letgolass for the 101th Elven Suicide Squad. They give your crossbow shots extra damage based off your EDR, and grant your bolts a chance to drill deep into an enemy.
A golden bracelet, essential equipment for knights to successfully rescue princesses from super ghosts and goblins.
A gauntlet of tempered arcanite, perfect for imbuing a warlock's attacks with energy while still affording protection to one's digits.
The palms of these gauntlets feel extremely warm. No flowers needed - let's get to throwing some fire.

Having evolved from the tried-and-tested 'leaf over groin' fashion style in the early days of elf-kind, these pieces of ceremonial attire are worn by hippiemancers during services to the Generic Mother Goddess.
A more practical solution for someone who wants to be armoured, yet retain some mobility. Not exactly short, but still relatively comfy and easy to wear.
Woven from golden threads, these pants are the height of fashion of wizards from a faraway land. Some curly-toed shoes would go wonderfully with these.
These pants may proclaim that you are indeed a bad dude (or dudette, for that matter), but are you bad enough to rescue the presiden- I mean, king?
Because wizards are simply too magical for pants.
Completely organic and one hundred percent cruelty-free.
After all you've been through, the fact that these things are caked in blood probably don't make a difference any more.

Designed by a sorceress to help her wizardly husband 'keep that knob of his where it belongs', the metal used to make these leggings does not naturally exist and has been painstakingly transmuted from radioactive metal and arcane crystals. The limited supply of these raw materials eventually led to great infighting amongst the metal farmers' consortium.

Initially developed for rapid building of fortifications overnight, these small crystals were later marketed to humans as cheap housing. Human adoption of this innovation was less smooth, though - early adopters tended to wall themselves in, resulting in a slow death from hunger and sleeping in one's own blue liquid.
Used to great effect in the civil war between the worms of armah geton, this bomb bursts into a small number of explosive clusters when thrown.
A strangely aerodynamic knight's lance. Throw this at ghouls and ghosts for best effect.
A strangely aerodynamic knight's lance. Throw this at enemies to knock them back.
A strangely aerodynamic knight's lance for gold-armoured knights. Throw this at ghouls and ghosts for best effect.
You are the milkman. Your milk is delicious. Special delivery today.
A flask of potent alchemical frostfire. This weapon does more damage the more skilled you are at alchemy.
A bottle of cooking oil, perfect for frying things with.

The Voltaic Core, a collaboration between the Starry Magician and the wizard Tesla, was the fruit of a lifetime's work and intended to give the common people access to cheap, plentiful voltaic energy. In the spirit of humanity, people started killing each other with it before the week was out.
When first developed, the mana grenade was unwieldy and largely too un-aerodynamic to be thrown effectively. This stopgap measure for effective delivery kind of stuck.
This dreaded bolt explodes into a mess of dragonsbreath caltrops when it hits anything.
This nasty bolt explodes into a mess of steel caltrops when it hits anything.
A small sliver of ice, perfect for freezing your enemies where they stand.
Not a perfect substitute for a magic missile, but you can still cast it into the darkness.
The very touch of these bolts turn your enemies to gold. As opposed to glod, which would be quite problematic.
These glimmering bolts have the ability to temporarily suppress their target's magics.
A medley of deadly devices and clockwork mechanisms, these bolts do more damage the more proficient you are at tinkering.
This bolt bursts into a massive blast of divine wrath. Stand well away!
This little gizmo allows you to temporarily take control of a construct - if the manual override manages to kick in, that is.
This odd bag of seeds will allow you to grow your own plant minions! Warning: requires a corpse to work. Dredmor Labs will not be held responsible if your new plant companion attempts to eat you.
This wand is extremely thick and solid. You'll be feeling the world shake before the day ends.
It's just a stick with moss for a handle. Or is it lichen? It's really hard to tell the difference.
The knob of this wand crackles with burning lightning, ready to discharge its magical load at any time.

As any necroeconomist will tell you, speculating in cthonic futures is a risky business. Play it safe by stacking physical life-force from the dead of your enemies - stand next to it and drain away! Remember, keep stacking physical!
When hungover, step on two of these and consult your physician if symptoms persist.
When activated, the mechanism in this trap sobers you up real good - a perfect excuse to drink more.
Formulated by the mechano-wizards of Binge, this amazing mechanism separates your (mostly) living flesh into one beaker, and all those nasty toxins dancing on your liver into another.
When activated, this trap releases a magical miasma that renders food and booze putrid and inedible.
When activated, this trap sprays slick, flammable oil all over the place.
When activated, this trap explodes into a wide field of magical frostfire.
Designed by Boyd Cooper to keep imaginary government agents off his lawn, this trap explodes in a blossom of flammable milk.
This trap removes you from the gene pool by turning you into something small, ugly and insignificant.
When activated, this trap releases a magical miasma that renders food and booze putrid and inedible. You could starve out a dwarf with this thing.

You're pretty sure this flask is actually an urn of some sort. The warm liquid within distinctly reminds you of phoenix down; swallowing the lot would probably cause you some pain, but you wonder just what might happen.
In the similar vein of ninja drugs and mustaches of power, it seems like someone's been able to extract the essence of pointy ears, haughty expressions and fake British accents into one small bottle.

The pages of this speech are crammed with miniscule handwriting. The opening address itself is approximately eighteen pages long, and by the looks of it this speech was intended to be given every time the bearer was attacked, hopefully boring any foes to death. How one would force said foes to sit through even so much as the address, though, is a mystery; a strange and utterly compelling force known as 'purplez' may have been involved.
You remember once seeing these at a garden party; no matter how many you took to stuff your face with, there always seemed to be more on the table. You vaguely wonder if that party's still going on.

This signet ring is wrought from silver and has a lovely ruby set in the middle. You've heard, though, that putting this on would send a message to the wrong crowd, ending up in you being 'farmed for rep'. Not that you know what that means, but it certainly sounds extremely painful.
Once a standard issue piece of equipment for the Blood Knights, this mana-draining pair of gauntlets is now a collector's item.
Fashioned from the corpses of interdimensional windchimes and available via mail order.
The insignia of a Blood Knight initiate. Wear it with something approaching pride!
This earring was once the height of elven fashion and unfortunately, restricted to the Blood Knights' battle-mage battalion. Of course, it's long gone out of use.
Ah, the memories of a youth spent in the beautiful gardens, raking in leaves and brains for the compost heap.
A gem of crystallised mana.
